Software Engineering.details: Learn core programming in one language (Java or Python): variables, control flow, OOP, collections, and debugging. Tools: IDE (IntelliJ/VS Code), Git basics. Project: CLI tool or small automation script. Timeline: 4–8 weeks.

Study arrays, lists, maps, trees, graphs, sorting, searching, Big-O. Practice on LeetCode/HackerRank at easy→medium. Skills: problem decomposition, complexity reasoning. Timeline: 8–12 weeks alongside projects.
Write tests (unit/integration), refactor safely, meaningful naming, code review mindset. Tools: JUnit/pytest, build tools (Maven/Gradle). Project: library with tests and CI on GitHub Actions.
REST principles, HTTP, JSON, validation, errors. Databases: SQL (PostgreSQL/MySQL), migrations, indexes. Project: CRUD service with auth-ready structure. Timeline: Intermediate phase.
Scalability concepts: caching, load balancing, queues, idempotency, observability. Read high-level designs of products you use. Discuss trade-offs (consistency vs availability) in writing.
Pick a track (backend, mobile, systems) and build a portfolio piece: production-minded repo, README, diagrams. Contribute to open source or internships. Timeline: ongoing Advanced growth.

Data Science.details: Python syntax, virtual environments, Jupyter. Libraries: NumPy, pandas. Git for notebooks. Project: clean and analyze a public CSV (Kaggle). Timeline: 4–6 weeks.
Distributions, hypothesis testing, confidence intervals, regression intuition. Resources: Khan/Stats courses, Think Stats. Skills: translate business questions into statistical tasks.
SQL joins, aggregations, window functions. Extract data from warehouses; document assumptions. Tools: PostgreSQL/SQLite, dbt optional. Project: analytical SQL + pandas pipeline.
scikit-learn: preprocessing, pipelines, cross-validation, metrics (precision/recall, RMSE). Move to supervised models first; then intro to deep learning if needed. Timeline: Intermediate.
matplotlib/seaborn/plotly; storytelling with charts. Summarize for non-technical stakeholders. Project: notebook with clear narrative + reproducible steps.
Model packaging, basic deployment (FastAPI + Docker), monitoring drift. Kaggle competitions or end-to-end projects with README. Timeline: 3–6+ months to strong portfolio.

Cybersecurity.details: OS fundamentals (Linux), TCP/IP, DNS, HTTP/S, firewalls. Set up a home lab (VMs). Tools: Wireshark basics. Timeline: 6–8 weeks.
CIA triad, threat modeling, IAM, cryptography concepts, OWASP Top 10. Skills: read CVE summaries responsibly; understand patching and hardening.
TryHackMe, HackTheBox (guided paths). Labs: DVWA, WebGoat on isolated VMs. Never test systems without authorization.
Blue team (SOC, SIEM, detection), GRC, appsec, or pentest. Align certs: Security+, CySA+, OSCP path later. Timeline: Intermediate.
Python/Bash for parsing logs, automating checks. Tools: Ansible basics, cloud security concepts (IAM policies).
Build write-ups (CTF-style), contribute to security tooling, internships. Stay ethical and document learning publicly (blog/GitHub) with redacted details.

Full Stack Development.details: HTML5, CSS (layout, flex/grid), responsive design, accessibility basics. JavaScript: DOM, fetch, async/await. Project: static + small interactive page. Timeline: 4–8 weeks.
React (or Vue): components, state, routing, forms. Tools: Vite, ESLint. Project: SPA consuming a public API.
REST, validation, auth concepts (JWT/sessions), error handling. Stack: Node/Express or Spring Boot. Project: secure CRUD API with tests.
Relational design, migrations, indexes. Use Prisma/Hibernate/JPA. Timeline: Intermediate full-stack apps.
Docker, environment config, CI pipeline, deploy to a PaaS. Observability: structured logs, health checks.
End-to-end product: auth, roles, payments optional, README, demo video. Performance and security review pass.

Cloud Computing.step: Step 1: Pick a cloud & core services (Beginner)
Cloud Computing.details: AWS, Azure, or GCP: IAM, VPC/VNet, compute (EC2/VM), object storage, managed DB overview. Free tier labs. Timeline: 6–10 weeks.
Terraform or Bicep/CloudFormation: modules, state, workspaces. Project: provision network + app stack reproducibly.
Docker images, multi-stage builds, registries. Kubernetes basics: pods, services, deployments. Tools: kubectl, minikube/kind.
Pipelines (GitHub Actions/GitLab), deploy to cloud, secrets management (vault/parameter store). Intermediate reliability practices.
Metrics, logs, traces; alerting. Cost optimization: rightsizing, reserved capacity, tagging. Skills: SLO thinking.
Multi-region, disaster recovery basics, zero-downtime deploys, security in cloud (least privilege, KMS). Certifications: AWS SAA/Azure AZ-104 path as you grow.
